architecture_summary: LeViT is a hybrid vision transformer for fast inference image classification that re-introduces convolutional components, uses a multi-stage pyramid with pooling/downsampling, and replaces explicit positional embeddings with learned attention bias. It is designed to optimize the speed/accuracy trade-off across GPU, Intel CPU, and ARM hardware.

benchmark_results.table_caption: Characteristics of LeViT w.r.t. two strong families of competitors: DeiT [3] and EfficientNet [17]. The top-1 numbers are accuracies on ImageNet or ImageNet-Real and ImageNet-V2 (two last columns). The others are images per second on the different platforms. LeViT models optimize the trade-off between efficiency and accuracy (and not #params). The rows are sorted by FLOP counts.

training_details.compute_surface: 32 GPUs that perform the 1000 training epochs in 3 to 5 days

novel_contributions: A multi-stage transformer architecture using attention as a downsampling mechanism
A computationally efficient patch descriptor that shrinks the number of features in the first layers
A learnt, per-head translation-invariant attention bias that replaces ViT’s positional embedding
A redesigned Attention-MLP block that improves the network capacity for a given compute time

key_claims.claim: LeViT significantly outperforms existing convnets and vision transformers with respect to the speed/accuracy tradeoff, and at 80% ImageNet top-1 accuracy it is 5 times faster than EfficientNet on CPU.

The paper’s contributions are a multi-stage transformer with attention-based downsampling, an efficient patch descriptor, learned per-head translation-invariant attention bias, and a redesigned Attention-MLP block.
LeViT-128S, LeViT-128, LeViT-192, LeViT-256, and LeViT-384 span a range of speed-accuracy tradeoffs by varying stage widths and depths.
LeViT is trained with distillation-driven training using two classification heads and a RegNetY-16GF teacher model on ImageNet.
LeViT-384 is on-par with DeiT-Small in accuracy but uses half the number of FLOPs, and LeViT-128S is on-par with DeiT-Tiny while using 4 × fewer FLOPs.
LeViT-192 and LeViT-256 have about the same accuracies as EfficientNet B2 and B3 but are 5 × and 7 × faster on CPU, respectively.
The ablation study shows that removing pyramid shape, PatchConv, BatchNorm, distillation, attention bias, wider blocks, or attention activation all degrades accuracy.
